text:
**What was missing and why MetaMask caught it but we didn't:**

MetaMask uses GoPlus's **address security** endpoint (`/api/v1/address_security/{address}`) — a completely different API from the token security endpoint (`/api/v1/token_security/{chainId}`). The token security endpoint analyzes contract mechanics (honeypot, taxes, ownership). The address security endpoint checks the address against **malicious contract databases, phishing registries, blacklists, sanctions lists, and cybercrime reports**. FRT's contract address was in one of those databases — that's what MetaMask's "Malicious" label comes from.

**What's now in place — full 5-layer pipeline:**

| Layer | What it catches |
|---|---|
| 1. Heuristic | Telegram links, "claim", URLs, pipe chars in name/symbol |
| 2. Moralis | Curated spam/airdrop token database |
| 3. GoPlus token security | Honeypot, airdrop scam, hidden owner, taxes, no liquidity |
| 4. Honeypot.is | Live tx simulation (ETH/BSC/Base) |
| **5. GoPlus address security** | **Malicious contract, phishing, blackmail, fake token, sanctions, cybercrime** |

**Pre-execution gate:** Even if a user manually re-selects a flagged token, clicking "Batch Swap" now runs a fresh full scan on all selected tokens. If anything comes back high-risk, execution is blocked, the token is auto-deselected, and the user sees exactly which token was blocked and why — before any wallet prompt appears.
